metadata.name: SELinux Port Labeling
metadata.description: Configure a web server to host content by using a nonstandard port
objectives: Configure a web server to host content by using a nonstandard port
prerequisites: Understanding of Apache web server configuration

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Web server running on nonstandard port 82

tasks.name: Verify port 82 has http_port_t SELinux context
tasks.description: SELinux must allow httpd to bind to port 82 by assigning the http_port_t context
tasks.success_message: Port 82 has the correct http_port_t SELinux context
tasks.failure_message: Port 82 does not have the http_port_t SELinux context
hints: Assign the SELinux port type: sudo semanage port -a -t http_port_t -p tcp 82
If port already exists with different type: sudo semanage port -m -t http_port_t -p tcp 82

tasks.name: Verify httpd service is running
tasks.description: The httpd service must be running to serve web content on port 82
tasks.failure_message: The httpd service is not running
hints: Restart the service: sudo systemctl restart httpd

tasks.name: Verify httpd is listening on port 82
tasks.description: The web server should be listening on the nonstandard port 82
hints: Restart httpd after SELinux changes: sudo systemctl restart httpd

tasks.name: Verify port 82 is open in firewall
tasks.description: The firewall must allow traffic on port 82/tcp for external access
tasks.success_message: Port 82/tcp is open in the firewall
tasks.failure_message: Port 82/tcp is not open in the firewall

tasks.name: Verify web content is accessible on port 82
tasks.description: The web server should serve content when accessed on port 82
tasks.success_message: Web content is accessible on port 82
tasks.failure_message: Web content is not accessible on port 82
hints: Check httpd status: systemctl status httpd
Check SELinux port context: semanage port -l | grep http_port_t
